title: maktaba-web-local — The Speaking Library (المكتبة الناطقة): offline multilingual RAG with a label-free per-tenant relevance gate
description: A fully offline, on-device, privacy-preserving, multilingual (Arabic + English) personal digital library with retrieval-augmented question answering. Its central contribution is an unsupervised, label-free, per-tenant self-calibrating relevance gate. This archive bundles the system code and four research artifacts: the flagship paper (per-tenant relevance gating with a conditional cross-lingual fallback) and three companion papers on (1) sparse-retrieval multi-tenant starvation and a per-tenant sub-index remedy, (2) a self-extending bilingual glossary for continual, label-free cross-script retrieval, and (3) safe deterministic post-hoc Modern-Standard-Arabic→Iraqi dialect rewriting. Every reported number is produced by deterministic experiment scripts included under papers/experiments/ and paper_experiments/. The private evaluation corpus (user-owned books) is not redistributed.

creators.name: Yousef, Ayman Kazim
creators.affiliation: Department of Artificial Intelligence Engineering, AlSafwa University, Karbala, Iraq

notes: Code is MIT-licensed; the papers (paper/ and papers/) are CC BY 4.0. Replace the GitHub URL placeholders in CITATION.cff and the papers' Data & Code Availability sections after the repository is published.
